overview.executiveSummary: Granola meetings reveal a decisive strategic pivot toward TDA + FlavorDB for the AI nutrition platform — you have technical direction, a collaborator (Sean), and momentum. OMI confirms this is your highest-energy initiative, plus recurring intent to expand team hours and formalize delegation. The real priorities: lock TDA architecture, capture 3 unactioned verbal commitments, clear CGM batch due Friday. Airtable shows 94% overdue but most are advisory artifacts — triage is system hygiene, not panic.
overview.redFlag: Your most important initiative (AI Nutrition Platform) exists only in conversations. One 15-min session to create an Airtable epic + Notion decision record would fix this entirely.

priorities.title: Lock TDA Architecture with Sean
priorities.detail: Bradley meeting set direction, OMI strongest signal

priorities.title: Capture 3 OMI Verbal Commitments
priorities.detail: Repeated across 4+ conversations, zero tasks exist

quickWins.text: Create "AI Nutrition Platform" epic in Airtable
Star/pin Nepal strategy doc in Notion

decisions: Core Vision Finalized: AI-powered diabetes-friendly meals tailored to cultural backgrounds. Mediterranean diet gap = core insight.
TDA Chosen: Topological Data Analysis over LLM prompting for scalability. Sean collaboration confirmed.
Three-tier validation: Community members, chefs, clinicians each providing different perspectives.

unactionedCommitments.detail: No task, no email, no budget check

themes.title: AI Nutrition Vision
themes.detail: Strongest signal. Clear platform vision around TDA + cultural food data.

themes.title: Resource Planning
themes.detail: You keep saying you want to increase team hours. Intent-action gap growing.

themes.title: Delegation Architecture
themes.detail: Want Jennifer to handle more, but no framework for her to step up.

tasks.title: Bloodborne Virus Clearance
tasks.description: Complete bloodborne virus clearance documentation for clinical work — compliance requirement.
tasks.claudePrompt:
I need to complete my bloodborne virus clearance for Stanford Medicine clinical rotations. Can you help me:
1. Draft an email to Stanford Occupational Health asking what documentation I need and if my existing records suffice
2. Create a checklist of typical bloodborne pathogen clearance requirements (Hep B titer, TB test, etc.)
3. Draft a follow-up reminder for 1 week from now if I haven't heard back

tasks.title: Team Hours Expansion
tasks.description: Recurring verbal commitment to increase Dalia's and Jennifer's hours — never actioned.
checklist: Message Dalia and Jennifer with proposed hours
tasks.claudePrompt:
I manage a research team at Stanford (NOURISH lab). I want to increase hours for two team members:
- Dalia: currently part-time, want to increase by ~5 hrs/week
- Jennifer: currently part-time, want to increase by ~5 hrs/week
Can you:
1. Draft a message to each person proposing the increase and asking their availability
2. Draft a budget justification paragraph I can send to my PI
3. Create a simple tracker for their updated weekly schedules

tasks.title: Ethics Assessment Submission
tasks.description: IRB modification mentioned in conversation — has a corresponding Airtable task.
tasks.claudePrompt:
I need to submit an IRB modification for my research study at Stanford. The study involves a nutrition education intervention with CGM data collection. Can you help me:
1. Create a checklist of typical IRB modification requirements
2. Draft the "Description of Changes" section
3. Review my modification for common rejection reasons and flag any issues

tasks.title: Jennifer Delegation SOP
tasks.description: You keep saying Jennifer should own more outreach — still no SOP or framework.
checklist: Define which tasks Jennifer should own
tasks.claudePrompt:
I want to delegate more outreach and communication tasks to my team member Jennifer at the NOURISH research lab. Help me create:
1. A delegation SOP document
2. An email/message template for standard outreach
3. A weekly check-in template (5-min async)
4. A "decision rights" matrix

tasks.title: Hackathon Follow-Up
tasks.description: Post-hackathon connections going cold. Action before they expire.
tasks.claudePrompt:
I recently participated in a hackathon and made several promising connections. Help me:
1. Draft 3 personalized follow-up emails
2. Create a simple CRM-style tracker
3. Suggest 2-3 concrete collaboration ideas

tasks.title: Case Report Draft
tasks.claudePrompt:
I'm drafting a clinical case report for publication. Can you help me:
1. Provide the standard CARE checklist structure
2. Draft an outline following journal submission guidelines
3. Write the Introduction section template
4. Suggest 3-5 target journals

tasks.title: Amazon Lab Supply Order
checklist: Place order through Stanford procurement or Amazon

ideas.pgQuoteTop: Live in the future, then build what's missing.
ideas.pgQuoteBottom: The best startup ideas are the ones that seem like bad ideas to most people.

ideas.title: The Cultural Plate
ideas.subtitle: AI Meal Designer That Respects Your Grandmother
ideas.essay: Every diabetes nutrition app recommends the Mediterranean diet. The problem: 85% of the world doesn't eat Mediterranean food. Your OMI conversations and Granola meetings reveal the real insight — flavor molecule networks (FlavorDB) can mathematically prove that culturally authentic substitutions are not just "OK alternatives" but are genuinely delicious pairings. Imagine an app where you say "I'm Tamil, diabetic, vegetarian" and it doesn't say "eat quinoa" — it says "replace white rice with barnyard millet in your sambar, add fenugreek for blood sugar control, and here's how your grandmother would have approved." The moat isn't the AI — it's the community-contributed recipe graph where every culture's food wisdom becomes network data.

ideas.title: The Competence Marketplace
ideas.subtitle: Uber for Institutional Knowledge
ideas.essay: Your OMI captured a striking observation: "the worsening ratio of competent to incompetent people" in organizations. What if competence itself is the scarce resource? Build a platform where the top 10% of performers in any domain can sell micro-consulting (15-minute blocks). A junior accountant at a startup gets 15 minutes with the best CFO at a Fortune 500 company. The pricing is dynamic. LinkedIn tells you who's competent. Nothing lets you rent that competence by the minute.

ideas.title: Flavor Roulette
ideas.subtitle: The Molecular Cooking Game
ideas.essay: A mobile game where players are given 3 random ingredients and must compose a dish. The twist: scoring is based on real flavor molecule overlap from FlavorDB. Players unlock cultural cuisine packs and discover that apparently bizarre combinations actually share deep molecular harmony. Level 2: add a diabetes constraint. Think Wordle meets MasterChef meets biochemistry. This could be the engagement layer for your research platform.

ideas.title: Glucose Poker
ideas.subtitle: Bet Your Biology
ideas.essay: A dinner party card game. Each player secretly wears a CGM. You eat the same meal, then bet on who spiked highest. Reveal the data. Identical meals produce wildly different responses — your friend's glucose might spike 3x yours from the same pasta. The educational payload is enormous but disguised as chaos.

ideas.title: The Competence Ratio
ideas.subtitle: Why Healthcare Fails at Scale
ideas.essay: Propose the "Competence Ratio" as a measurable metric: in any system, there's a threshold below which the system doesn't degrade linearly but collapses exponentially. A team of 10 with 8 competent people outperforms a team of 100 with 30 — not by 3x but by 30x. The solution to healthcare workforce shortages isn't "hire more people" but "ruthlessly protect the competence ratio."

ideas.title: The Grandmother Hypothesis
ideas.subtitle: Of Nutrition Science
ideas.edge: You're literally building the tools to prove this
ideas.essay: What if grandmother knowledge is actually a distributed clinical trial running across millions of households for centuries? Traditional food wisdom is an unstructured dataset of n=billions. Modern tools (NLP, FlavorDB, CGM) can finally extract the signal. When you run the numbers, grandmothers are right about 70-80% of their claims. The remaining 20% reveals cultural-specific metabolic adaptations we didn't know existed.

ideas.title: The Decision Fossil Record
ideas.subtitle: Never Lose Context Again
ideas.essay: Build a tool that passively records why decisions were made (from meeting transcripts + voice notes) and creates a "fossil record." Six months from now, when someone asks "why did we choose TDA over LLM prompting?", the tool surfaces the exact conversation. Organizations don't fail from bad decisions. They fail from forgetting why good decisions were made.

ideas.title: The Nutrition Colonialism Map
ideas.subtitle: A world map visualization
ideas.essay: Each country colored by what percentage of its traditional cuisine appears in global nutrition databases. France, Italy, Japan: 80%+. India, Nigeria, Ethiopia, Peru: under 5%. Overlay with diabetes prevalence. The correlation is devastating and immediate. Animate as a time-lapse. Submit to The Lancet as a visual essay.

ideas.title: The 20-Minute Thesis
ideas.subtitle: What If Every PhD Took 20 Minutes?
ideas.essay: Constrain every research question to: "Can I get a meaningful signal in 20 minutes of data collection?" For 30 days, capture one micro-thesis per day from voice notes, spend exactly 20 minutes testing it, publish the results. The bottleneck in science isn't rigor — it's the absurd overhead between having an insight and testing it.

dataSources.name: Granola
dataSources.desc: Recurring meeting topics, repeated action items, process descriptions

dataSources.name: Airtable
dataSources.desc: Tasks with high "Times Delayed", recurring patterns, work stream bottlenecks

dataSources.name: OMI
dataSources.desc: Voice notes: "every week I...", "I always have to...", delegation intent

dataSources.name: Notion
dataSources.desc: Process docs, SOPs, templates, repeated page structures

candidates.name: Meeting Action Item Extraction
candidates.pattern: After every meeting, 3-5 action items discussed but only ~40% make it into Airtable.

candidates.name: OMI Commitment Capture
candidates.pattern: Verbal commitments in OMI conversations go unactioned 60%+ of the time.

candidates.name: Weekly Status Report Generation
candidates.pattern: Stefan synthesizes weekly updates manually from multiple sources.

candidates.name: CGM Participant Follow-Up Templating
candidates.pattern: CGM participant communications follow the same structure every time.

candidates.name: Notion Decision Record Writer
candidates.pattern: Key decisions happen in meetings but rarely get documented in Notion.

crossSourcePatterns.title: Meeting → Task → Doc Pipeline
crossSourcePatterns.desc: Granola meetings generate action items → become Airtable tasks → need Notion docs. Currently manual at every step.
crossSourcePatterns.flow: Granola → manual entry → Airtable → manual write → Notion

crossSourcePatterns.title: Voice → Task Capture
crossSourcePatterns.desc: OMI captures verbal commitments that aren't making it into Airtable.
crossSourcePatterns.flow: OMI voice → lost → should be → Airtable task

crossSourcePatterns.title: Grant Cycle Loop
crossSourcePatterns.desc: IRB feedback from meetings triggers Notion doc updates, which generate new Airtable tasks.
crossSourcePatterns.flow: Granola meeting → IRB feedback → Notion update → Airtable task
